metadata.description: Custom Claude Code plugins — multi-phase AI development pipeline (essense-flow + autopilot), data exploration, prompt modifiers, project question tracking, cross-platform alerts, and verifiability-aware work classification.

plugins.name: essense-flow
plugins.description: Multi-phase AI development pipeline. Closed contracts, evidence-bound review, fail-soft hooks, no resource caps. Eleven skills (elicit, research, triage, architect, organize, build, glossary, review, verify, context, heal) drive a state machine from project pitch to shipped code. /organize (post-architect) and /glossary (post-build) are optional DRY phases powered by the plugin-toolkit code-glossary engine; /glossary renders a functionality map (MAP.md) plus per-sprint duplication drift, /architect consults the map before deciding (reuse ledger in ARCH.md), /build dispatches carry existing-helper + neighbor context, and /review gains a glossary-fed dry-violation lens. Every phase-producing skill writes artifact + transitions state atomically. State is artifacts-authoritative: .pipeline/state.yaml is a derived cache that self-reconciles from disk (state-reconcile op; missing cache auto-rebuilds inside ops). Every agent self-report is re-validated against disk — drift is auditable, not silent. Producer agents follow the librarian protocol: what they cannot verify they research, what research cannot answer becomes a structured unknowns[] entry surfaced to the user at phase gates — never assumed. Hooks are advisory: context-inject surfaces phase + canonical paths on every prompt, next-step suggests the recommended slash command on Stop. Both fail-soft on degraded state — never block tool calls. v0.7.0 archived on archive/essense-flow-v0.7.

plugins.name: essense-autopilot
plugins.description: Stop-hook autopilot for essense-flow pipelines. Drives the pipeline forward across phases without you typing between steps. Halts at human gates (eliciting, organizing, glossarying, verifying), real blockers, in-flight background Agent calls (transcript-scan), forward-detect for sprint-complete + QA-REPORT, and no-progress (same phase + sprint + wave as last fire â†’ suggests /heal). Opt-in per project via .pipeline/config.yaml. Diagnostic stderr on every halt path.

plugins.name: schema-scout
plugins.description: CLI tool for exploring the schema and values of any data file (XLSX, CSV, JSON). Auto-detects JSON in cells, repairs double-encoded UTF-8, prunes overflow artifacts.

plugins.name: thorough-mode
plugins.description: Prompt modifiers — keyword triggers (++, @thorough, @ship, @present, @debug, @verify, @fresh, @prompt, @build) that inject behavioral rules for thoroughness, documentation checks, interactive questions, root-cause investigation, paranoid verification, context refresh, next-session kickoff-prompt generation, and plan-review-build change workflows. Smart hints suggest modifiers when you describe the intent without the keyword. @thorough, @fresh, and @prompt injections are protocol-shaped (failure named -> ordered steps -> anti-signals -> exit check); @prompt DRAFT->VERIFY->COLD-READ->SAVE disk-verifies every citation and SAVES each prompt to an append-only .claude/prompts/ history + INDEX.md ledger. @build REVIEW carries a reuse-first gate (already built here, or served by a package? reuse/extend, do not reinvent). @ship integrates with plugin-toolkit (/version-bump + /docs-audit) when used in mk-cc-resources plugin repo.

plugins.name: project-note-tracker
plugins.description: Track questions per handler/department. Auto-detects which handler should answer, researches from project context in the background, logs to an Excel tracker, generates meeting agendas. Auto-gitignores itself.

plugins.name: alert-sounds
plugins.description: Cross-platform alerts for Claude Code events — sound, desktop notifications, status line colors, volume control, and mute/unmute. Fully configurable per event.

plugins.name: session-lifecycle
plugins.description: Session lifecycle tools — handoff (capture session state), resume (restore context from handoff), claude-md-sync (propose CLAUDE.md updates after changes), retro (metrics-driven retrospective), meta-review (diagnose session: multi-step chains, skill friction, plugin coverage gaps). Designed as cohesive set: handoff triggers claude-md-sync when stale, resume reads handoff output, meta-review diagnoses what's worth automating from observed patterns. Handoffs are an append-only history — each /handoff writes a permanent timestamped file + INDEX.md ledger under .claude/handoffs/ (latest mirrored to .claude/handoff.md for /resume); /resume preserves the history instead of truncating it.

plugins.name: plugin-toolkit
plugins.description: Plugin/skill development + maintenance toolkit. /skill-heal audits a plugin's skill set against current best practices (parallel review agents, scorecard, diagnostic only). /plugin-scaffold bootstraps a new plugin with full directory + cross-references (9-step chain in one invocation). /version-bump cascades version updates across plugin.json + marketplace.json + bundle + metadata + RELEASE-NOTES with semver validation. /docs-audit cross-checks CLAUDE.md + README + marketplace.json against disk state and proposes targeted fixes. /code-glossary (v2) audits any codebase for DRY violations — a deterministic Python engine (Python/TS/JS/C# via stdlib AST + tree-sitter) indexes every function, fingerprints 5 signals, and clusters duplicates; in-session sub-agents label against a 147-verb controlled vocabulary, review each cluster (Pass B), substrate-verify instances (Pass C), and judge near-miss candidates generated deterministically. Optional block scanner (--scan-blocks) surfaces duplicated sub-function guard patterns. Writes GLOSSARY.yaml (frozen schema, /dry-refactor input) + GLOSSARY.md; `runner diff` tracks duplication drift between runs by {(file, function)} identity; `runner map` renders MAP.md — a functionality map (mermaid module graph + lossless machine index) consulted before designing or building; `runner coupling` enforces DECOUPLED by measuring coupling on a scope-aware call graph from records — threshold-free binary violations (cross-module dependency cycles + reach-ins into a module's internal surface), `--fail-on-violation` CI gate, COUPLING.yaml names each violation file:function for substrate-verification. /dry-refactor (v3 MVP) turns extractable clusters into reviewable refactor plans: 7 Appendix-A pre-flight gates (baseline tests, git-clean, target module, verification, confidence, substrate-verify, gitignore) + dry-run helper/edit-plan output, zero source writes. Also powers essense-flow's /organize + /glossary phases. Composable — @ship references /version-bump + /docs-audit; /skill-heal hints at /docs-audit when description quality is weak.

plugins.name: verifiability-lens
plugins.description: A strict, opinionated work-quality guardian that fires automatically on your work and pushes it to the best achievable. Runs THREE checks and ACTIVELY verifies (web + docs + read tools — confirms/refutes, not just flags): (1) Verifiability — A (verified/checkable) / B (genuine guess) / U (can't tell; never let a U pass as A); (2) Completeness — was everything meant to be done actually done, or did we stop arbitrarily (presses to continue + finish, tested); (3) Quality bar — tested, requirements met, robust, not half-assed. A surfacing triage (auto-resolve | escalate | suppress) tuned by a recipient profile hands you ONLY the important, actionable, fully-contextualized pushes — strict judgment, disciplined surfacing; never a context-less decision; auto-resolutions logged. Fires via an opt-in Stop hook (OFF by default) on substantive-work turns (code/files, reads, web, subagents, MCP), reading the WHOLE turn; pure questions + its own output never trigger it. Also a /verifiability manual trigger. Read/research-only — verifies and pushes, does not write code or run the build. Carries a hook — install separately from the mk-cc-all bundle.

plugins.name: reuse-gate
plugins.description: Reuse-first reminder at the moment code is written. A PreToolUse hook fires ONCE per user message on the first source-code write (Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit) and surfaces the reuse-first checklist — is this already implemented here (search the codebase / functionality glossary MAP.md) or served by an existing package/library? reuse or extend before writing new; what you do write, build modular + decoupled. A deterministic nudge at the exact point of writing, so the rule can't drift out of context the way an instruction can. Mechanism: hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext fires once per user message (dedupe keyed on prompt_id) on the first source write of the turn — injected before the tool runs so the write proceeds normally (no block, no permissionDecision, so your normal write-permission prompt is untouched). Source-only — docs/config/data files never trigger. Opt-in OFF by default (env REUSE_GATE_ENABLED=1 / project or global .claude/reuse-gate.json {"enabled": true}, project overrides global); fail-open on any error. Companion to the reuse-first instructions in essense-flow code-conventions.md, global CLAUDE.md, and thorough-mode @build. Carries a hook — install separately from the mk-cc-all bundle.

plugins.name: mk-cc-all
plugins.description: All currently-active mk-cc-resources skills in one install: essense-flow, schema-scout, project-note-tracker, session-lifecycle, plugin-toolkit. essense-autopilot, thorough-mode, alert-sounds, verifiability-lens, and reuse-gate carry hooks and must be installed separately. Benched plugins (miltiaze, ladder-build, architect, safe-commit, project-structure, repo-audit, mk-flow) are preserved on the archive/benched-plugins branch.
